name: Vero GTM Week-1 Dashboard
purpose: Monday-morning Slack digest covering the six numbers that tell whoever ran the engine last week whether to ship, kill, or double a variant.

metrics.label: Weekly send volume
metrics.sourceQuery: sequence.touches WHERE sent_at >= last_monday AND sent_at < this_monday
metrics.notes: Snapshot is cut once per week so the trend line doesn't get noisy.

metrics.label: Reply rate
metrics.notes: Excludes auto-replies and bounces from numerator and denominator.

metrics.label: Demo book rate
metrics.notes: Counts only first-touch demos; reschedules don't double-count.

metrics.label: Trial start rate
metrics.notes: Trial start triggers a Stripe customer event; HubSpot is the source of truth for demo completion.

metrics.label: ARR added
metrics.sourceQuery: SUM(subscriptions.annual_value) WHERE first_paid_at BETWEEN last_monday AND this_monday
metrics.notes: Counted at first successful charge, not at trial start. Annualised from monthly ACV.

metrics.label: Time-to-first-touch (median)
metrics.notes: Measured in business minutes, not wall-clock. Auto-pause windows (overnight, weekend) are subtracted.

segments.label: Reply rate by EMR cohort
segments.rationale: Variance across cohorts is the single most actionable signal we get.

segments.label: Reply rate by city tier
segments.rationale: Tells us whether T1 saturation is depressing the global number or the rest is.

segments.label: Open rate by subject-line variant
segments.rationale: Drives the next A/B kill or double decision.

qualitativeSignal.label: Top 5 positive replies, copy-pasted
qualitativeSignal.rule: Sales reads these out loud in Tuesday review. This is the signal the dashboard does not surface.
qualitativeSignal.redaction: Lead names replaced with first-name-only; clinic names redacted unless customer.

alerts.action: Pause queue. Page GTM engineer in #gtm-alerts.
Pause new sequence variants. Founder review.
Notify in #gtm. Auto-create Linear ticket against the cron.
